question: What is DeltaNet (linear attention)? How does it differ from softmax attention?
code_hint: Linear attention: phi(K)^T * phi(Q) * V, circumventing softmax O(n^2)
expected_output: DeltaNet: O(n*d^2) vs softmax attention O(n^2*d), linearizes attention
template:
# Softmax Attention: O(n^2 * d) - all pairs computed
# Linear Attention: O(n * d^2) - reorders computation
# Key: Use kernel feature map phi() to enable linearization
# DeltaNet uses delta vectors to capture differences

print("DeltaNet: O(n*d^2) vs softmax attention O(n^2*d), linearizes attention")
answer:
# Softmax Attention: O(n^2 * d) - all pairs computed
# Linear Attention: O(n * d^2) - reorders computation
# Key: Use kernel feature map phi() to enable linearization
# DeltaNet uses delta vectors to capture differences

print("DeltaNet: O(n*d^2) vs softmax attention O(n^2*d), linearizes attention")
